hero.title: Software development in Morocco, secure by design
hero.intro: We design and build your websites, business platforms and mobile applications with a double standard: flawless user experience, and security woven into every line of code.

problem.title: Your challenge
problem.text: Successful software is not just functional: it must be fast, maintainable, scalable — and safe. Too many projects sink under invisible technical debt, drifting deadlines or vulnerabilities discovered too late. Our answer: short cycles, proven technology choices and systematic security reviews.

steps.title: Scoping & workshops
steps.text: Business understanding, scope, prioritized user stories and a transparent estimate.

steps.title: UX/UI & prototyping
steps.text: Figma mockups tested with your users before a single line of code is written.

steps.title: Iterative development
steps.text: Two-week Scrum sprints, regular demos, continuous integration and cross code reviews.

steps.title: Quality & security
steps.text: Automated tests, OWASP checks, security review before every production release.

steps.title: Go-live & maintenance
steps.text: Deployment, monitoring, corrective and evolutionary maintenance with service commitments.

points.title: Certified where it matters
points.text: Oracle Java, Scrum PSM, SAFe, AWS/Azure/OCI cloud: certifications held by the developers who actually work on your project.

points.title: Security in our DNA
points.text: Tachfir means “encryption”. Every application is reviewed by our cybersecurity practice before going live.

points.title: Close and responsive
points.text: A single point of contact and answers within 24–48 h, in Morocco and for your teams in France.

items.q: What kinds of projects do you build?
items.a: Corporate websites and e-services, business web platforms (management tools, portals, dashboards), iOS/Android mobile apps, rebuilds of existing applications and API integrations. We work with public administrations as well as SMEs and larger companies.

items.q: Which technologies do you use?
items.a: Mainly React/Next.js on the front end, Spring Boot (Java) on the back end, AWS or Azure hosting, and Figma for design. The final choice depends on your context: we always favor maintainability and the availability of skills on the market.

items.q: How does a typical project run?
items.a: A one-to-two-week scoping phase, then two-week development sprints with a demo at every iteration. You see the product move forward continuously and can reprioritize at each sprint.

items.q: How much does custom development cost?
items.a: It depends on the scope. After a first exchange, we send a range-based estimate within 24–48 h, then a detailed quote after scoping. No hidden costs: everything is written down.

items.q: Do you provide maintenance after go-live?
items.a: Yes. Our application maintenance contracts cover corrective work, evolutions and monitoring, with response-time commitments matched to the criticality of your application.

items.q: How is security handled?
items.a: Systematic code reviews, OWASP best practices, secrets management, encryption of sensitive data and an internal penetration test before go-live for critical applications. It is our trademark.

related.title: Go further
related.article: Read: nearshore in Morocco for French companies

hero.title: Cybersecurity & pentesting in Morocco: test your defenses
hero.intro: Penetration tests, security audits and remediation support, delivered by certified consultants. Find your vulnerabilities before someone else does.

problem.title: Your challenge
problem.text: Attacks don't send a warning: ransomware, data leaks, account takeovers. For an administration or a company, the question is no longer whether you will be targeted, but when — and how prepared you will be. A regular penetration test turns that uncertainty into a concrete action plan.

steps.title: Scoping & rules of engagement
steps.text: Scope, scenarios (black, grey, white box), intervention windows and written authorizations.

steps.title: Reconnaissance & mapping
steps.text: Inventory of the attack surface: exposed services, applications, configurations, accounts.

steps.title: Controlled exploitation
steps.text: Real but contained intrusion attempts, without disrupting production, with evidence.

steps.title: Report & remediation plan
steps.text: Executive summary for management, technical detail for your teams, prioritized by actual risk.

steps.title: Retest
steps.text: Verification after remediation: identified flaws are properly closed, with a final report.

deliverables.title: Your deliverables
items: Executive report readable by a board
Detailed technical report with proof of exploitation
Test attestation (usable for your clients and tenders)

points.title: Field certifications
points.text: Cisco CyberOps Associate, CCNA — our consultants understand the infrastructure they test, not just the attack tools.

points.title: Actionable reports
points.text: No raw scanner output: every vulnerability is verified, contextualized and paired with a concrete fix.

points.title: Contractual confidentiality
points.text: Systematic NDA, data processed in Morocco, Law 09-08 compliance. Your test results never leave the agreed circle.

items.q: What is the difference between a penetration test and a security audit?
items.a: An audit assesses your overall posture (configurations, policies, architecture) through documentation and technical analysis. A penetration test simulates a real attack to prove what an attacker could actually achieve. They complement each other.

items.q: How often should we test?
items.a: At least once a year, and after every major change (new application, infrastructure overhaul, opening a service to the public). Organizations handling sensitive data usually test every six months.

items.q: Can the test disrupt our production systems?
items.a: No. The rules of engagement define intervention windows, excluded systems and forbidden techniques. Destructive tests are never run without explicit agreement and a dedicated environment.

items.q: What does the report contain?
items.a: An executive summary (overall risk level, trends), the detail of each vulnerability with proof of exploitation, its criticality in context, and concrete remediation recommendations. A retest then verifies the fixes.

items.q: Do you work with the public sector?
items.a: Yes. We answer purchase orders and tenders from administrations and public institutions, with compliant invoicing (ICE, RC) and reinforced confidentiality clauses.

items.q: Do you follow recognized methodologies?
items.a: Our tests build on OWASP references (web and mobile) and PTES good practices, adapted to your context. Every engagement is documented and reproducible.

related.title: Go further
related.article: Read: the most in-demand IT certifications in Morocco

hero.title: Nearshore IT consulting in Morocco — the right profiles for Morocco and France
hero.intro: Staff augmentation, project assistance, application maintenance: we strengthen your teams with certified consultants, in Morocco or remotely for your projects in France. No heaviness, transparent reporting.

problem.title: Your challenge
problem.text: Hiring a good developer or an experienced project manager takes months — and your projects won't wait. For French companies there is also the cost equation: the talent exists, but not always at the right rate. Moroccan nearshore answers both: certified skills, available quickly, in your time zone and your language.

steps.title: Need qualification
steps.text: Skills, seniority, project context, duration: we scope precisely before proposing.

steps.title: Pre-screened profiles
steps.text: Qualified CVs and technical interviews organized quickly — you validate, we commit.

steps.title: Guided onboarding
steps.text: Structured onboarding, secured environment, daily check-ins during the first weeks.

steps.title: Steering & reporting
steps.text: Regular activity reports, agreed KPIs, monthly governance meeting.

steps.title: Controlled scale-up
steps.text: Team extension, knowledge transfer or reversibility: everything is contractual.

nearshoreArgs.title: Nearshore Morocco: the winning equation for France

items.title: Aligned time zone
items.text: 0 to 1 hour difference with Paris: your teams work in real time, not in shifts.

items.title: Native French
items.text: Meetings, documentation, code reviews: everything happens in French, without friction.

items.title: Optimized costs
items.text: Rates significantly below equivalent services in France, with equally certified skills.

items.title: Clear framework
items.text: Adapted contracts, GDPR clauses, confidentiality and intellectual property locked down.

deliverables.title: How we engage
items: Staff augmentation: one or more consultants embedded in your teams
Project assistance: scoping, requirements, project steering
Dedicated nearshore team managed by Tachfir

points.title: Certified consultants
points.text: PSM, SAFe, Java, cloud, Kubernetes: our teams' certifications are verifiable — and verified by our most demanding clients.

points.title: Used to large accounts
points.text: Intelcia, Proxiad, Alten, SFR: we know the quality and reporting requirements of large organizations.

points.title: Committed for the long run
points.text: Real mission follow-up, managed replacement if needed, and one goal: every consultant should bring more than the job description.

items.q: What is the difference between staff augmentation and fixed price?
items.a: In staff augmentation, you manage the consultant embedded in your teams and pay for time spent. In fixed price, we commit to a result, a deadline and a price. Application maintenance often combines both: a fixed base plus work units.

items.q: Why choose nearshore in Morocco over far offshore?
items.a: Same time zone as Paris (0–1 h difference), fluent French, easy travel (a 3-hour flight), a close working culture and a legal framework proven by hundreds of French companies already operating here. You keep the responsiveness of a local team, at nearshore cost.

items.q: How quickly can a consultant start?
items.a: For common skills (Java/React development, DevOps, QA), we usually present profiles within one to two weeks. The actual start depends on your validation processes and the access to be provisioned.

items.q: How do you handle data protection (GDPR / Law 09-08)?
items.a: Standard contractual clauses for transfers, named and logged access, segregated environments, consultant awareness training. Morocco has its own framework (Law 09-08, CNDP) which we apply natively.

items.q: What kinds of profiles do you provide?
items.a: Front/back/full-stack developers, DevOps and cloud engineers (AWS, Azure, OCI, Kubernetes), cybersecurity consultants, QA engineers, PSM/SAFe-certified Scrum Masters and project managers, and business analysts.

items.q: Can we start small before scaling?
items.a: We even recommend it: many of our clients start with one consultant or a small maintenance scope, then extend the team once trust and processes are in place.

items.title: Networking & security

items.title: Printing & scanning

items.title: Software licenses

items.title: Consumables & accessories

publicSector.title: Built for public buyers
items: Response to purchase orders and calls for tenders
Compliant invoicing: ICE, RC, contract references

steps.title: Your request
steps.text: Send your needs, PO or specifications via the form, by email or WhatsApp.

steps.title: Quote within 24–48 h
steps.text: You receive a detailed quote: references, prices, lead times, warranty.

steps.title: Validation
steps.text: Purchase order or contract award — we launch procurement.

steps.title: Delivery & installation
steps.text: On-site delivery, installation and commissioning if requested, acceptance report.

steps.title: Invoicing & support
steps.text: Invoice compliant with your process (ICE, RC), warranty and support ensured.

items.q: Why is there no online catalog?
items.a: Because IT hardware prices and availability change constantly, and every organization has its own constraints (compatibility, warranty, budget). A tailored quote within 24–48 h is more reliable than an outdated catalog.

items.q: Who can order from Tachfir?
items.a: Public administrations and institutions (by purchase order or contract), private companies and associations. We provide the administrative documents required by each purchasing process.

items.q: What are your lead times?
items.a: The quote is sent within 24–48 working hours. Delivery times depend on the references and are stated — and committed — in the quote.

items.q: How are warranty and after-sales service handled?
items.a: All hardware is covered by the manufacturer's warranty, with extensions available. In case of failure, we handle the diagnosis and the replacement or repair process.

items.q: Do you answer calls for tenders?
items.a: Yes. We prepare complete bid files (administrative, technical, financial) and meet the requirements of Moroccan public procurement: references, ICE, RC, certificates.

cta.title: A hardware need? Let's price it.
cta.text: Attach your purchase order, specifications or a simple list of needs: a detailed quote comes back within 24–48 h.

hero.title: Tachfir: trust, encrypted
hero.intro: “Tachfir” (تشفير) means encryption in Arabic. The name is no accident: security has been our DNA since day one.

story.title: Our story
story.text1: Tachfir was born in Morocco from a simple observation: organizations don't need more IT vendors, they need vendors they can trust. Teams that deliver what they promise, secure what they build and pass on what they know.
story.text2: Today, more than 30 certified experts support international IT firms, telecom operators, public administrations and SMEs — in Morocco and France — across four practices: development, cybersecurity, consulting and training, complemented by IT hardware supply.

items.title: Trust
items.text: Earned through action: written commitments, transparency on costs and deadlines, code you own.

items.title: Security
items.text: Our name says it: every deliverable goes through the security filter, whichever practice produced it.

items.title: Rigor
items.text: Certifications kept up to date, cross reviews, continuous improvement: the bar is not negotiable.

items.title: Transmission
items.text: We train our clients in what we practice — a learning company is a stronger company.

team.title: The team
team.text: Developers, pentesters, cloud and DevOps consultants, trainers: a team of 30+ experts with verifiable certifications, led by engineers who still write code.
team.note: [PENDING — team photos and bios to be provided]

sections.title: Site publisher
Hosting
sections.text: The site is hosted by Vercel Inc., 440 N Barranca Ave #4133, Covina, CA 91723, USA — vercel.com. Content (database, media) is hosted on secured cloud infrastructure.

sections.title: Intellectual property
sections.text: All site content (texts, visuals, logos, graphic elements) is the property of Tachfir or its partners and is protected by Moroccan law and international intellectual property conventions. Any unauthorized reproduction is prohibited. Third-party trademarks and logos (clients, vendors) remain the property of their respective owners.

sections.title: Personal data
sections.text: The processing of personal data collected through this site is described in the Privacy policy, in accordance with Law No. 09-08 on the protection of individuals with regard to the processing of personal data and CNDP guidelines.

sections.title: Applicable law
sections.text: This site and its legal notice are governed by Moroccan law. Any dispute falls under the jurisdiction of Moroccan courts.

privacyPage.title: Privacy policy
privacyPage.intro: Tachfir attaches particular importance to the protection of your personal data. This policy describes the data we collect through www.tachfir.com, how we use it and your rights, in accordance with Moroccan Law No. 09-08 and the guidelines of the CNDP (the Moroccan data protection authority).

sections.title: Data controller
sections.text: Tachfir, {address}, {city} — Email: {email}. The processing of data collected through this site is subject to the required formalities with the CNDP. [PENDING — CNDP receipt number]

sections.title: Data we collect
sections.text: Through our forms (quote, purchase order, contact): identity (name, role), contact details (email, phone), organization (company name, sector, ICE where applicable), the content of your request and attached files (purchase order, specifications). Through analytics: anonymized browsing data (if you accept it).

sections.title: Purposes and legal basis
sections.text: This data is used exclusively to: answer your quote and contact requests, prepare the commercial documents requested, and follow up on the business relationship. Legal basis: your consent (checkbox at submission) and pre-contractual measures taken at your request.

sections.title: Recipients
sections.text: Your data is intended for Tachfir's sales and technical teams. It transits through our technical processors: site and database hosting (Vercel, Neon), email delivery (Resend). It is never sold, rented or transferred to third parties for commercial purposes.

sections.title: Retention period
sections.text: Data related to a request is kept for as long as needed to process it, then for a maximum of 3 years after the last contact for business follow-up, unless a longer legal retention applies (contractual and accounting documents).

sections.title: Your rights
sections.text: In accordance with Law 09-08, you have the right to access, rectify and object to the processing of your data. To exercise it, write to {email} describing your request; we will answer as soon as possible. You may also contact the CNDP (www.cndp.ma).

sections.title: Cookies and analytics
sections.text: The site only uses technical cookies (theme and language preference) and, where enabled, Google Analytics 4 audience measurement. You can configure your browser to refuse non-essential cookies without affecting navigation.

sections.title: Security
sections.text: Exchanges with the site are encrypted (HTTPS/TLS). Access to data is restricted, named and logged. Attached files are transmitted directly to the relevant team and are not publicly exposed.
